marketName: Data Processing, Hosting, and Related Services

assessments.rationale: Data centers have NFPA 101 / EN 179 fire-code mandated emergency exits. Product's single-motion release pre-satisfies both EU and US requirements.
Power-independent mechanical alarm is an asset in data centers where power events (UPS switchover, generator fail) are routine operational scenarios.
Hyperscale DCs (10K-100K m2) and dense metallic RF environments attenuate 868 MHz signal. Multiple IP gateways required. Not a physics knockout — standard repeater architecture resolves coverage.
assessments.mitigation: Deploy IP Gateway / Protocol Bridge per DC security zone (~30 m coverage radius per gateway); Add 868 MHz wireless repeater stages for coverage extensions; Conduct site RF survey during commissioning

assessments.rationale: DC emergency exits are genuine emergency-only access points with very low event frequency. Battery life comfortably exceeds 2-year target.

DC emergency exits are in stairwells, service corridors, and perimeter walls where surface mount is standard. Flush-mount requirements apply only to server hall access doors controlled by biometric PACS.
Data centers require door events in enterprise PACS and SIEM for compliance certifications. EasyWave is incompatible with REST/SNMP/BACnet-IP natively. IP Gateway bridges the gap.
assessments.mitigation: Deploy GfS IP Gateway translating EasyWave events to REST / SNMP / BACnet-IP; Integrate door-event stream into enterprise PACS; Configure SIEM ingestion for SOC 2 CC6.4 physical security audit evidence

assessments.rationale: Per-door cost ~EUR 730 is negligible for DC security budgets. Automated SOC 2 / ISO 27001 audit log generation delivers clear compliance ROI.
EU data centers must cover employee physical access monitoring in DPIA. DC operators are compliance-sophisticated — extension of current program, not new capability.
assessments.mitigation: Extend existing DPIA to cover door-event monitoring; Execute DPA with monitoring SaaS provider; Implement data minimization — event type + timestamp only

assessments.rationale: DC server halls operate at ASHRAE A1/A2 conditions (18-27C, 40-60% RH, dust-controlled). No washdown or wet exposure. Standard IP class is sufficient.

DCs integrate emergency exits with EM door holders and biometric access control. Combined configurations require EN 13637 system certification. System integrator owns this; standard practice for DC commissioning.
assessments.mitigation: System integrator certifies combined configuration to EN 13637; GfS provides module-level compliance declaration; Include combined-system certification in commissioning documentation
